jokes.text: Well, I got a pair of eights. / Ah. Sorry about that, bro, I got a Dolly Parton. / What's that? / A bigger pair. Whaa!
jokes.setup: Carlton claims to have a 'Dolly Parton' in a card game
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to mean 'a bigger pair' - referring to both cards and Dolly Parton's famous anatomy
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre with good setup/reveal structure. The 'Whaa!' sells it well.

jokes.text: Oh, man, I could just kiss you. / I could just kiss you too.
jokes.setup: Will expresses gratitude enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Carlton's earnest response creates an awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Carlton's earnestness creating awkwardness is classic character work, but the joke itself is simple.

jokes.text: Next time I'm gonna let Jazz kiss you.
jokes.setup: Carlton's awkward kiss comment earlier
jokes.punchline: Will threatens Carlton with an even more awkward scenario
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the awkwardness while staying true to Will's character.

jokes.text: The Strip 'N Trip wanna know if the hotel will furnish extension cords? And tell Naughty Nelvina and The Naked Drill Team to wear coats or they'll never get past hotel security.
jokes.setup: Jazz is planning entertainment for the bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and inappropriate entertainment names
jokes.explanation: Creative naming and perfect character integration for Jazz's over-the-top planning style.

jokes.text: How could I miss that big square head of yours?
jokes.setup: Will is blindfolded and bumps into Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will's insult about Carlton's head shape
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with a mild insult punchline.

jokes.text: Oh, no, not another Scooby-Doo piñata. / Pssh. I wish.
jokes.setup: Will dreads another childish party decoration from Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton's 'I wish' suggests his actual party is even more disappointing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Carlton's complete disconnect from cool party planning.

jokes.text: No offense, Scrappy... but it's hard to top the parties I've been to.
jokes.setup: Will is being diplomatic about Carlton's party planning
jokes.punchline: The nickname 'Scrappy' (Scrappy-Doo) reinforces Carlton's childish image
jokes.explanation: Good character nickname that reinforces the Scooby-Doo reference and Carlton's earnest nature.

jokes.text: Go, Will. Go, Will. Go, Will.
jokes.setup: Will brags about his party experiences
jokes.punchline: Cut to flashback of Will being cheered at a party
jokes.explanation: Standard flashback gag supporting Will's party reputation.

jokes.text: Girl, I'm sure glad we got rid of that nut.
jokes.setup: Continuing the party flashback
jokes.punchline: A woman dismissing Will as 'that nut' undermines his cool image
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of Will's party story, showing he may not be as popular as he claims.

jokes.text: I was trying to get up to my room. / Hey, Tom, what was you doing? / I was trying to be in the video.
jokes.setup: Will and Jazz are caught doing something inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Random guy Tom was just trying to get in their 'video'
jokes.explanation: Random character's innocent motivation contrasts with Will and Jazz's antics.

jokes.text: Man, this party is dull. / No, man, this party is stupid. / No, no, no, this party is crazy, flat, dumb, dumb, doodie.
jokes.setup: Will and Jazz criticize Carlton's party
jokes.punchline: Carlton's childish attempt to out-insult them with 'doodie'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - trying to be tough but using a childish insult that reveals his nature.

jokes.text: You're the man. You're the man. / You're the man. / You're the man. / BOTH: Ahhh!
jokes.setup: The three are hyping each other up
jokes.punchline: Their synchronized yell and presumably ridiculous behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy moment without much wit.

jokes.text: Jazz is the best man? / Then what the heck am I? / Short.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what his role is if not best man
jokes.punchline: Jazz's blunt answer about Carlton's height
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with great economy. Jazz's directness creates the humor.

jokes.text: He got me Rolanda, the Rubber Lady. / Look, I'll have you know that Stan, the world's smartest duck... was this close to getting on Leno.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains about Jazz's entertainment choice
jokes.punchline: Jazz's absurd defense of his terrible booking involving a duck
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd entertainment booking that's perfectly in character for Jazz's terrible planning.

jokes.text: Yeah, let's have a moment of silence... for all the girls that have found me irresistible.
jokes.setup: Jazz suggests a solemn moment for Will's bachelorhood ending
jokes.punchline: Will's self-absorbed interpretation focusing on his supposed appeal to women
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's ego, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: Mirror, mirror on the wall, Jean-Claude Van Damme, I'm fine.
jokes.setup: Will admiring himself in a mirror
jokes.punchline: Mixing fairytale reference with Van Damme's accent saying 'damn'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining pop culture references with Will's vanity.

jokes.text: You know, if somebody was to walk in here right now... they might think you was hitting on me. / Trust me, if I was hitting on you, you'd know It.
jokes.setup: Will suggests an awkward interpretation of their interaction
jokes.punchline: Lisa's confident response implying her hitting on someone would be unmistakable
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with sexual tension, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Really? How? / [Kiss demonstration] / Okay, you made your point.
jokes.setup: Will asks how he'd know if Lisa was hitting on him
jokes.punchline: Lisa presumably demonstrates with an aggressive kiss
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to verbal setup, works but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Which one of these losers is my date? / That would be me. / This is my cousin, Carlton. / Where's the rest of him?
jokes.setup: Woman asks about her date, Will introduces Carlton
jokes.punchline: Her blunt question about Carlton's height
jokes.explanation: Another height joke about Carlton, economical but not particularly original.

jokes.text: I may be short, but you know what they say... about short men and tall women, don't you? / No. What? / I don't know, just making conversation.
jokes.setup: Carlton starts what sounds like a confident pickup line
jokes.punchline: He admits he has no actual point, just awkward small talk
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of pickup line expectation, perfectly captures Carlton's awkward earnestness.

jokes.text: Man, didn't your mothers ever tell you, don't play with buttons?
jokes.setup: The elevator breaks after Carlton and Jazz mess with buttons
jokes.punchline: Will's parental scolding about button-pushing
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about common childhood advice.

jokes.text: Can I throw a party or what? / I don't believe this. / There's a python and some triplets in there... and I'm stuck in here with Ren and Stumpy.
jokes.setup: Jazz brags about his party while they're stuck in elevator
jokes.punchline: Will's frustration at missing the good party, calling Carlton and Jazz cartoon characters
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference characterizing Carlton and Jazz as the cartoon duo, captures Will's frustration.

jokes.text: We've only been in here for five minutes, Carlton. Get a grip.
jokes.setup: Carlton panics about survival in the elevator
jokes.punchline: Will points out they've barely been stuck at all
jokes.explanation: Standard reality check joke about Carlton's tendency to panic.

jokes.text: I am not fine, all right? I'm a grown man... sitting under the table, hugging a teddy bear. I'm not fine.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims he's not fine after the earthquake
jokes.punchline: The visual of grown Carlton hiding under table with teddy bear
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy combining Carlton's panic with infantile behavior, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Y'all going to leave me hanging up here like a piece of Rotisserie Gold, huh?
jokes.setup: Will is apparently suspended or stuck somehow
jokes.punchline: His comparison to 'Rotisserie Gold' (likely meaning rotisserie chicken)
jokes.explanation: Creative and quotable metaphor that's distinctly Will's voice and speaking style.

jokes.text: Oh, so I guess we're going down, huh? / Faster than a bucket of chicken at Oprah's.
jokes.setup: Will realizes they're in a falling elevator
jokes.punchline: His pop culture reference to Oprah's famous love of food
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference with great economy, perfectly timed for tension relief.

jokes.text: Let me out! Let me out! / This is all your fault, you finger-licking ragamuffin.
jokes.setup: Carlton panics and blames Jazz
jokes.punchline: Carlton's oddly specific and antiquated insult 'finger-licking ragamuffin'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific insult that perfectly captures Carlton's preppy vocabulary even in crisis.

jokes.text: Who are you calling muffin, Little Debbie?
jokes.setup: Jazz responds to being called 'ragamuffin'
jokes.punchline: His misunderstanding and comeback calling Carlton 'Little Debbie'
jokes.explanation: Good callback wordplay on 'muffin' and creative insult comparing Carlton to the snack cake mascot.

jokes.text: Wow, gravity. What a rush.
jokes.setup: They just survived a falling elevator
jokes.punchline: Will's casual comment about experiencing gravity
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to life-threatening situation, very economical.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'd rather be anybody but myself right now. / Well, it certainly wouldn't be the first time.
jokes.setup: Will wishes he was someone else in this situation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dig at Will's history of pretending to be other people
jokes.explanation: Good character callback to Will's tendency to assume false identities, sets up next scene.

jokes.text: Now, where did you transfer from? / Bend over. / It's Andover. We're going back home.
jokes.setup: Will is pretending to be a prep school student
jokes.punchline: Will mispronounces 'Andover' as 'Bend over'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation joke that reveals Will's unfamiliarity with prep school culture.

jokes.text: That's a fake mustache. / No, it's not. / Ah! Yes, it is. / No, it's not.
jokes.setup: Woman discovers Will's disguise
jokes.punchline: Will's stubborn denial despite being caught
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy with Will's characteristic stubborn denial.

jokes.text: Will? / Well, it ain't Fat Albert.
jokes.setup: Phil recognizes Will despite his disguise
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response referencing the cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that's distinctly Will's voice, economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Look, at least you got a chance to live life to it's fullest. / I only had one woman. One.
jokes.setup: Will tries to comfort Carlton about their impending death
jokes.punchline: Carlton's admission of extremely limited romantic experience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Carlton's inexperience, creates sympathy and humor.

jokes.text: Will you marry me? / You are too adorable for words. / Can I give you a kiss goodbye? / Yes. / Tomorrow morning.
jokes.setup: Carlton proposes in desperation, woman finds him cute
jokes.punchline: She agrees to a kiss but delays it, showing she's not really interested
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of romantic desperation - she's kind but not actually interested.

jokes.text: When the elevator was falling and I thought we were gonna die... your life flashed before my eyes.
jokes.setup: Carlton reflects on their near-death experience
jokes.punchline: Instead of his own life, he saw Will's life flash before his eyes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the 'life flashing before your eyes' cliché with perfect character logic.

jokes.text: Girl, you look good, I wish I could plant you and grow a whole field of y'all.
jokes.setup: Will demonstrates his pickup techniques to Carlton
jokes.punchline: Creative agricultural metaphor as a pickup line
jokes.explanation: Creative and distinctly Will-style pickup line that's both smooth and absurd.

jokes.text: Girl, I got to tell you. That suit look like a piece of good God, wrapped up with some have mercy... with a side of oomph!
jokes.setup: Will continues demonstrating his pickup style
jokes.punchline: Elaborate food-service metaphor using religious exclamations
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly over-the-top Will pickup line mixing religious language with restaurant service.

jokes.text: I've seen your picture before. / Oh, yeah, that's right. It was in the dictionary next to 'ka-blam!'
jokes.setup: Classic pickup line setup about seeing someone's picture
jokes.punchline: Unexpected dictionary reference with comic book sound effect
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of tired pickup line with unexpected and energetic punchline.

jokes.text: Well, I'm hoping you like it dark, hot... sweet and with a spoon in It.
jokes.setup: Will offers cappuccinos to women
jokes.punchline: His suggestive description of how he hopes they like their coffee
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo delivered in Will's style, competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Yo, baby, I know your feet must be tired... because you've been running through my mind all day!
jokes.setup: Will approaches woman with concern about her feet
jokes.punchline: Classic cheesy pickup line about running through his mind
jokes.explanation: Extremely well-known pickup line, gets credit for quotability but lacks originality.

jokes.text: Hold, hold, hold it, baby. See, now, I noticed you noticing me. So I just want to put you on notice that I noticed you too.
jokes.setup: Will tries another approach with the woman
jokes.punchline: Convoluted wordplay with 'notice/noticed/noticing'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay but overly complicated, typical of Will's sometimes too-clever approach.

jokes.text: Is that what passes for a compliment around here? / Actually, baby, you got it, but I take donations.
jokes.setup: Woman criticizes Will's pickup attempt
jokes.punchline: Will's arrogant response that she's lucky but he accepts charity
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Will's inability to handle rejection, perfectly arrogant response.

jokes.text: She tied a pork chop around your neck just so the dog would play with you.
jokes.setup: Jazz joins the insult battle
jokes.punchline: Classic 'so ugly' joke about needing meat to get attention from pets
jokes.explanation: Well-known insult formula, gets points for quotability but lacks originality.

jokes.text: Well, you so ugly, your mama had to feed you with a slingshot.
jokes.setup: Will responds to Jazz's insult
jokes.punchline: Creative variation on 'so ugly' jokes involving feeding method
jokes.explanation: Creative twist on standard insult format, showing Will's verbal skills.

jokes.text: You're so ugly, that ain't a fade on your head... that's your hair running away from your face.
jokes.setup: Will continues the insult battle
jokes.punchline: Creative explanation for Jazz's haircut as hair literally escaping
jokes.explanation: Excellent creative insult with specific visual imagery, stands out from typical formula.

jokes.text: Strip away the shoulder pads and the muscles and the dimples... and what you got? / You.
jokes.setup: Woman describes what makes someone attractive
jokes.punchline: Carlton's self-deprecating punchline
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that's perfectly in character for Carlton's insecurity.

jokes.text: Carlton. Carlton, man, I'm sorry about all those short jokes. You know, it really took a big man to handle it... and, well, you tried your best, okay?
jokes.setup: Will apologizes for making fun of Carlton's height
jokes.punchline: His apology contains another height joke ('big man') and backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Will can't help himself even when trying to be sincere.

jokes.text: Geoffrey? / But you told me your name was Will. / No, I said I was willing.
jokes.setup: Woman is confused about Will's identity
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's smooth wordplay explanation
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy and character integration for Geoffrey's smooth nature.

jokes.text: Not really. All I could think about was you.
jokes.setup: Woman asking if Will's life flashed before his eyes
jokes.punchline: Will's romantic response about thinking of her instead
jokes.explanation: Sweet romantic moment but not particularly funny - more aww than laugh.
